57: /**

59:  * Searches for multiple comment formats — both old and new pipeline versions.
60:  */
61: function parseFailureFromComments(
62:   ctx: InspectorContext,
63:   issueNumber: number,
64: ): { failedStage: string; failedError: string } {
65:   try {
66:     const comments = ctx.github.getIssueComments(issueNumber)

68:     for (let i = comments.length - 1; i >= 0; i--) {
69:       const body = comments[i].body

72:       if (body.includes('\u274c Pipeline failed') || body.includes('Pipeline failed')) {

151:   }
152: 
153:   // Completed

155:     return {
156:       ...task,
157:       health: 'completed',
158:       healthDetail: 'Pipeline completed successfully',
159:     }
160:   }

162:   // Failed or timeout
163:   if (status.state === 'failed' || status.state === 'timeout') {
164:     let failedStage = 'unknown'
165:     let failedError = ''
166:     for (const [stageName, stage] of Object.entries(status.stages || {})) {

173:     return {
174:       ...task,
175:       health: 'failed',

177:       failedStage,
178:       failedError,
179:     }
180:   }
181: 
182:   // Paused (gated)
183:   if (status.state === 'paused') {
184:     let pausedStage = 'unknown'
185:     let gatedMinutes = 0

280: export function createDigestAction(

287:   }

289:   // Only include actionable tasks (not completed, not unknown/done)
290:   const actionable = tasks.filter((t) => t.health !== 'completed' && t.health !== 'unknown')
291: 
292:   // If nothing actionable, skip digest entirely
293:   if (actionable.length === 0) {
294:     return null
295:   }
296: 
297:   const healthCounts: Record<TaskHealth, number> = {
298:     healthy: 0,
299:     completed: 0,
300:     stalled: 0,
301:     failed: 0,
302:     gated: 0,
303:     orphaned: 0,
304:     unknown: 0,
305:   }
306:   for (const task of tasks) {
307:     healthCounts[task.health]++
308:   }
309: 
310:   return {
311:     plugin: 'cody-health-check',
312:     type: 'digest',
313:     urgency: 'info',
314:     title: 'Cody Pipeline Health Digest',
315:     detail: `Failed: ${healthCounts.failed} | Running: ${healthCounts.healthy} | Gated: ${healthCounts.gated} | Completed: ${healthCounts.completed}`,
316:     dedupKey: 'digest',
317:     // FIX #7: Increase dedup to 6 hours — digest is rarely changing
318:     dedupWindowMinutes: 360,
319:     execute: async () => {
320:       // Read fixer state for context
321:       const fixerState =
322:         ctx.state.get<Record<string, { retries: number; fixIssueNumber: number | null }>>(
323:           'cody:fixerState',
324:         ) || {}
325: 
326:       // Summary line
327:       let md = `## 🐕 Inspector Digest — Cycle ${ctx.cycleNumber}\n\n`
328:       md += `**Summary**: ${healthCounts.failed} failed, ${healthCounts.healthy} running, ${healthCounts.gated} gated, ${healthCounts.completed} completed, ${healthCounts.unknown} unknown\n\n`
329: 
330:       // Only show actionable tasks
331:       if (actionable.length > 0) {
332:         md += `### Actionable Tasks\n\n`
333:         md += `| Issue | Task | Status | Detail | Fixer |\n|-------|------|--------|--------|-------|\n`
334: 
335:         for (const task of actionable) {
336:           const emoji =
337:             task.health === 'healthy'
338:               ? '🟢'
339:               : task.health === 'failed'
340:                 ? '🔴'
341:                 : task.health === 'stalled'
342:                   ? '🟡'
343:                   : task.health === 'gated'
344:                     ? '🟠'
345:                     : task.health === 'orphaned'
346:                       ? '👻'
347:                       : '❓'
348: 
349:           const fixer = fixerState[task.taskId]
350:           const fixerInfo = fixer
351:             ? `retry ${fixer.retries}/5${fixer.fixIssueNumber ? ` • fix #${fixer.fixIssueNumber}` : ''}`
352:             : '—'
353: 
354:           md += `| #${task.issueNumber} | ${task.taskId} | ${emoji} ${task.health} | ${task.healthDetail.slice(0, 60)} | ${fixerInfo} |\n`
355:         }
356:       }
357: 
358:       md += `\n_${new Date().toISOString()}_`
359: 
360:       ctx.github.postComment(ctx.digestIssue!, md)
361:       return { success: true, message: 'Digest posted' }
362:     },
363:   }
364: }
365: 
366: /**
367:  * Health check plugin.
368:  *
369:  * Responsibilities:
370:  * - Discover active Cody tasks
371:  * - Evaluate health (healthy, stalled, failed, gated, orphaned, completed)
372:  * - Create nudge actions for gated tasks
373:  * - Create digest action for visibility
374:  * - Share evaluated tasks via state for pipeline-fixer plugin
375:  *
376:  * NOTE: Failed task retries are delegated to the pipeline-fixer plugin.
377:  */
378: export const healthCheckPlugin: InspectorPlugin = {
379:   name: 'cody-health-check',
380:   description: 'Monitor Cody pipeline health and take corrective action',
381:   domain: 'cody',
382: 
383:   async run(ctx) {
384:     ctx.log.debug('Running health-check plugin')
385: 
386:     const tasks = await discoverTasks(ctx)
387:     ctx.log.debug({ taskCount: tasks.length }, 'Discovered tasks')

392:     ctx.state.set('cody:evaluatedTasks', evaluated)
393: 
394:     const actions: ActionRequest[] = []
395: 
396:     for (const task of evaluated) {
397:       const nudge = createNudgeAction(task, ctx)
398:       if (nudge) actions.push(nudge)
399:     }
400: 
401:     const digest = createDigestAction(evaluated, ctx)
402:     if (digest) actions.push(digest)
403: 
404:     ctx.log.debug({ actionCount: actions.length }, 'Generated actions')

<content>1: /**
2:  * @fileType plugin
3:  * @domain inspector
4:  * @pattern pipeline-fixer
5:  * @ai-summary Retry failed tasks, escalate persistent failures by creating fix-issues for Cody
6:  *
7:  * Flow:
8:  *   Retry 1-2: Simple rerun from failed stage with error as feedback
9:  *   Retry 3 (same error): Create a GitHub issue describing the failure, trigger @cody on it
10:  *   Retry 4-5: Rerun original task (now with potentially fixed pipeline code)
11:  *   Give up after 5 attempts
12:  *
13:  * Cross-task dedup: Before creating a fix issue, checks if an open fix issue
14:  * already exists for the same stage failure. If so, adds a comment instead
15:  * of creating a duplicate issue.
16:  */
17: 
18: import type {
19:   InspectorPlugin,
20:   ActionRequest,
21:   InspectorContext,
22:   EvaluatedTask,
23: } from '../../../core/types'
24: import { readTaskFile } from '../../../clients/github'
25: import { getQueueState } from '../queue-manager/queue-state'
26: 
27: // ============================================================================
28: // Types
29: // ============================================================================
30: 
31: interface FixerTaskState {
32:   retries: number
33:   errorSignature: string
34:   fixIssueNumber: number | null
35:   fixIssueCreatedAt: string | null
36: }
37: 
38: type FixerState = Record<string, FixerTaskState>
39: 
40: // ============================================================================
41: // Constants
42: // ============================================================================
43: 
44: const STATE_KEY = 'cody:fixerState'
45: const MAX_RETRIES = 5
46: const FIX_ISSUE_THRESHOLD = 2
47: const DEDUP_WINDOW_MINUTES = 15
48: const FIX_ISSUE_LABEL = 'cody:pipeline-fix'

51: // Helpers
52: // ============================================================================
53: 
54: // FIX #8: When stage is 'unknown', default to empty string so pipeline decides
55: function resolveFromStage(stage: string): string {
56:   if (stage === 'unknown') return ''
57:   if (stage === 'commit') return 'commit'
58:   if (stage === 'pr') return 'pr'
59:   if (stage === 'verify' || stage === 'autofix') return 'build'
60:   return stage
61: }
62: 
63: function errorSignature(stage: string, error: string): string {
64:   return `${stage}:${error.slice(0, 200).trim()}`
65: }
66: 
67: function isNonRetryable(error: string): boolean {
68:   const lower = error.toLowerCase()
69:   return (
70:     lower.includes('api key') ||
71:     lower.includes('_api_key') ||
72:     lower.includes('enospc') ||
73:     lower.includes('no space left') ||
74:     lower.includes('disk full')
75:   )
76: }

78: function getFixerState(ctx: InspectorContext): FixerState {
79:   return ctx.state.get<FixerState>(STATE_KEY) ?? {}
80: }
81: 
82: function getTaskState(state: FixerState, taskId: string): FixerTaskState {
83:   return (
84:     state[taskId] ?? {
85:       retries: 0,
86:       errorSignature: '',
87:       fixIssueNumber: null,
88:       fixIssueCreatedAt: null,
89:     }
90:   )
91: }
92: 
93: function saveFixerState(ctx: InspectorContext, state: FixerState): void {
94:   ctx.state.set(STATE_KEY, state)
95: }
96: 
97: // FIX #14: Prune fixer state entries older than 7 days (based on fixIssueCreatedAt or retries stale)
98: function pruneFixerState(state: FixerState): FixerState {
99:   const MAX_ENTRIES = 100
100:   const entries = Object.entries(state)
101:   if (entries.length <= MAX_ENTRIES) return state
102: 
103:   // Keep only the most recent MAX_ENTRIES entries
104:   const pruned: FixerState = {}
105:   const sorted = entries.sort((a, b) => (b[1].retries || 0) - (a[1].retries || 0))
106:   for (const [key, value] of sorted.slice(0, MAX_ENTRIES)) {
107:     pruned[key] = value
108:   }
109:   return pruned
110: }

115:  */

117:   stage: string,
118:   currentTaskId: string,
119:   fixerState: FixerState,
120: ): number | null {
121:   for (const [taskId, taskState] of Object.entries(fixerState)) {
122:     if (taskId === currentTaskId) continue
123:     if (taskState.fixIssueNumber && taskState.errorSignature.startsWith(stage + ':')) {
124:       return taskState.fixIssueNumber
125:     }
126:   }
127:   return null
128: }

130: /**
131:  * Link a task to an existing fix issue: post comments and update state.
132:  */
133: function linkToExistingFixIssue(
134:   ctx: InspectorContext,

147:     '',
148:     'Error:',
149:     errorSnippet,
150:   ].join('\n')

152:   ctx.github.postComment(existingIssueNumber, linkComment)
153: 
154:   const newRetries = taskState.retries + 1

156:     ...taskState,
157:     retries: newRetries,
158:     fixIssueNumber: existingIssueNumber,
159:     fixIssueCreatedAt: taskState.fixIssueCreatedAt,
160:   }
161:   fixerState[task.taskId] = newTaskState
162:   saveFixerState(ctx, pruneFixerState(fixerState))

166:     existingIssueNumber +
167:     ' (same stage failure).'
168:   ctx.github.postComment(task.issueNumber, issueComment)

170:   ctx.log.info(

229: function createRetryAction(
230:   task: EvaluatedTask,
231:   taskState: FixerTaskState,
232:   fixerState: FixerState,
233: ): ActionRequest {
234:   const failedStage = task.failedStage || 'unknown'
235:   const fromStage = resolveFromStage(failedStage)
236:   const retryNum = taskState.retries + 1
237:   const fixRef = taskState.fixIssueNumber ? ` after fix issue #${taskState.fixIssueNumber}` : ''
238: 
239:   return {
240:     plugin: 'cody-pipeline-fixer',
241:     type: 'retry',
242:     target: task.taskId,
243:     urgency: 'critical',
244:     title: `Retry ${retryNum}/${MAX_RETRIES} for ${task.taskId}`,
245:     detail: `Retrying from ${fromStage || 'auto'}${fixRef}`,
246:     dedupKey: `pipeline-fixer:${task.taskId}`,
247:     dedupWindowMinutes: DEDUP_WINDOW_MINUTES,
248:     async execute(ctx: InspectorContext) {
249:       const feedback =
250:         readTaskFile(task.taskId, 'verify.md').slice(0, 2000) ||
251:         readTaskFile(task.taskId, `${failedStage}.md`).slice(0, 2000) ||
252:         task.failedError ||
253:         ''
254: 
255:       // FIX #3: Pass issue_number in dispatch payload
256:       const inputs: Record<string, string> = {
257:         task_id: task.taskId,
258:         mode: 'rerun',
259:         issue_number: String(task.issueNumber),
260:         feedback: feedback.slice(0, 2000),
261:       }
262:       if (fromStage) inputs.from_stage = fromStage
263: 
264:       ctx.github.triggerWorkflow('cody.yml', inputs)
265: 
266:       ctx.github.postComment(
267:         task.issueNumber,
268:         `🔄 **[pipeline-fixer: retry ${retryNum}/${MAX_RETRIES}]** Retrying from \`${fromStage || 'auto'}\`${fixRef}`,
269:       )
270: 
271:       // Update state
272:       const newTaskState: FixerTaskState = {
273:         ...taskState,
274:         retries: retryNum,
275:         errorSignature: errorSignature(failedStage, task.failedError || ''),
276:       }
277:       fixerState[task.taskId] = newTaskState
278:       saveFixerState(ctx, pruneFixerState(fixerState))
279: 
280:       ctx.log.info({ taskId: task.taskId, retry: retryNum, fromStage }, 'Triggered retry')
281:       return { success: true, message: `Retry ${retryNum} triggered from ${fromStage || 'auto'}` }
282:     },
283:   }
284: }
285: 
286: function createFixIssueAction(
287:   task: EvaluatedTask,
288:   taskState: FixerTaskState,
289:   fixerState: FixerState,
290: ): ActionRequest {
291:   const failedStage = task.failedStage || 'unknown'
292:   const errorSummary = (task.failedError || 'Unknown error').slice(0, 80)
293: 
294:   return {
295:     plugin: 'cody-pipeline-fixer',
296:     type: 'create-fix-issue',
297:     target: task.taskId,
298:     urgency: 'critical',
299:     title: `Create fix issue for ${task.taskId}`,
300:     detail: `Pipeline fails at ${failedStage} after ${taskState.retries} retries`,
301:     dedupKey: `pipeline-fixer:${task.taskId}`,
302:     dedupWindowMinutes: DEDUP_WINDOW_MINUTES,
303:     async execute(ctx: InspectorContext) {
304:       const issue = ctx.github.getIssue(task.issueNumber)
305:       const issueBody = issue.body || ''
306: 
307:       // Cross-task dedup: check if another task already created a fix issue for the same stage
308:       const existingFixFromState = findExistingFixIssue(failedStage, task.taskId, fixerState)
309:       if (existingFixFromState) {
310:         return linkToExistingFixIssue(
311:           ctx,
312:           task,
313:           taskState,
314:           fixerState,
315:           existingFixFromState,
316:           failedStage,
317:         )
318:       }
319: 
320:       // Also search GitHub for open fix issues with a similar title
321:       const fixSearchQuery = [
322:         '"[pipeline-fix]"',
323:         '"fails at ' + failedStage + '"',
324:         'label:' + FIX_ISSUE_LABEL,
325:         'is:open',
326:       ].join(' ')
327:       const searchResults = ctx.github.searchIssues(fixSearchQuery)
328:       if (searchResults.length > 0) {
329:         return linkToExistingFixIssue(
330:           ctx,
331:           task,
332:           taskState,
333:           fixerState,
334:           searchResults[0].number,
335:           failedStage,
336:         )
337:       }
338: 
339:       // Create a new fix issue
340:       const title = `[pipeline-fix] Cody fails at ${failedStage}: ${errorSummary}`
341:       const body = buildFixIssueBody(task, taskState, issueBody)
342:       const labels = [FIX_ISSUE_LABEL]
343: 
344:       const fixIssueNumber = ctx.github.createIssue(title, body, labels)
345: 
346:       // FIX #2: Always advance retries even if createIssue fails,
347:       // so we don't get stuck in an infinite loop trying to create the same issue
348:       const newRetries = taskState.retries + 1
349:       const newTaskState: FixerTaskState = {
350:         ...taskState,
351:         retries: newRetries,
352:         fixIssueNumber: fixIssueNumber ?? taskState.fixIssueNumber,
353:         fixIssueCreatedAt: fixIssueNumber ? new Date().toISOString() : taskState.fixIssueCreatedAt,
354:       }
355:       fixerState[task.taskId] = newTaskState
356:       saveFixerState(ctx, pruneFixerState(fixerState))
357: 
358:       if (!fixIssueNumber) {
359:         ctx.log.error(
360:           { taskId: task.taskId, retries: newRetries },
361:           'Failed to create fix issue — retries advanced',
362:         )
363:         return { success: false, message: `Failed to create fix issue (retries now ${newRetries})` }
364:       }
365: 
366:       // Trigger Cody on the fix issue
367:       ctx.github.postComment(fixIssueNumber, '@cody')
368: 
369:       ctx.github.postComment(
370:         task.issueNumber,
371:         `🔧 **[pipeline-fixer]** Created fix issue #${fixIssueNumber} — Cody will analyze and fix the pipeline.`,
372:       )
373: 
374:       ctx.log.info({ taskId: task.taskId, fixIssueNumber }, 'Created fix issue and triggered Cody')
375:       return { success: true, message: `Created fix issue #${fixIssueNumber}` }
376:     },
377:   }
378: }
379: 
380: function createGiveUpAction(task: EvaluatedTask, fixerState: FixerState): ActionRequest {
381:   return {
382:     plugin: 'cody-pipeline-fixer',
383:     type: 'give-up',
384:     target: task.taskId,
385:     urgency: 'warning',
386:     title: `Giving up on ${task.taskId}`,
387:     detail: `Exhausted ${MAX_RETRIES} retry attempts`,
388:     dedupKey: `pipeline-fixer:${task.taskId}`,
389:     dedupWindowMinutes: 60 * 24,
390:     async execute(ctx: InspectorContext) {
391:       const fixRef = fixerState[task.taskId]?.fixIssueNumber
392:         ? ` + fix issue #${fixerState[task.taskId].fixIssueNumber}`
393:         : ''
394: 
395:       ctx.github.postComment(
396:         task.issueNumber,
397:         `⛔ **[pipeline-fixer]** Exhausted ${MAX_RETRIES} retry attempts${fixRef}. Manual intervention required.`,
398:       )
399: 
400:       delete fixerState[task.taskId]
401:       saveFixerState(ctx, fixerState)
402: 
403:       ctx.log.warn({ taskId: task.taskId }, 'Gave up after max retries')
404:       return { success: true, message: 'Gave up — manual intervention required' }
405:     },
406:   }
407: }
408: 
409: function createNonRetryableAction(task: EvaluatedTask): ActionRequest {
410:   return {
411:     plugin: 'cody-pipeline-fixer',
412:     type: 'non-retryable',
413:     target: task.taskId,
414:     urgency: 'warning',
415:     title: `Non-retryable failure: ${task.taskId}`,
416:     detail: task.failedError || 'Infrastructure failure',
417:     dedupKey: `pipeline-fixer:non-retryable:${task.taskId}`,
418:     dedupWindowMinutes: 60 * 24,
419:     async execute(ctx: InspectorContext) {
420:       ctx.github.postComment(
421:         task.issueNumber,
422:         `🚫 **[pipeline-fixer]** Non-retryable infrastructure failure detected. Fix the environment and rerun manually.\n\n**Error:** ${task.failedError || 'Unknown'}`,
423:       )
424:       ctx.log.warn({ taskId: task.taskId, error: task.failedError }, 'Non-retryable failure')
425:       return { success: true, message: 'Non-retryable notice posted' }
426:     },
427:   }
428: }
429: 
430: // ============================================================================
431: // Plugin
432: // ============================================================================
433: 
434: export const pipelineFixerPlugin: InspectorPlugin = {
435:   name: 'cody-pipeline-fixer',
436:   description: 'Retry failed tasks, escalate persistent failures by creating fix-issues for Cody',
437:   domain: 'cody',
438: 
439:   async run(ctx) {
440:     ctx.log.debug('Running pipeline-fixer plugin')
441: 
442:     const evaluated = ctx.state.get<EvaluatedTask[]>('cody:evaluatedTasks') || []
443:     const failed = evaluated.filter((t) => t.health === 'failed')
444: 
445:     if (failed.length === 0) {
446:       ctx.log.debug('No failed tasks')
447:       return []
448:     }
449: 
450:     const queueState = getQueueState(ctx)
451:     const activeQueueTaskId = queueState.activeTaskId
452: 
453:     const fixerState = getFixerState(ctx)
454:     const actions: ActionRequest[] = []
455: 
456:     for (const task of failed) {
457:       if (activeQueueTaskId && task.taskId === activeQueueTaskId) {
458:         ctx.log.debug({ taskId: task.taskId }, 'Skipping queue-managed active task')
459:         continue
460:       }
461: 
462:       if (!task.issueNumber || task.issueNumber <= 0) {
463:         continue
464:       }
465: 
466:       const taskState = getTaskState(fixerState, task.taskId)
467:       const currentSig = errorSignature(task.failedStage || 'unknown', task.failedError || '')
468: 
469:       if (isNonRetryable(task.failedError || '')) {
470:         actions.push(createNonRetryableAction(task))
471:         continue
472:       }
473: 
474:       if (taskState.retries >= MAX_RETRIES) {
475:         actions.push(createGiveUpAction(task, fixerState))
476:         continue
477:       }
478: 
479:       // Phase 1: Simple retries (0-1)
480:       if (taskState.retries < FIX_ISSUE_THRESHOLD) {
481:         actions.push(createRetryAction(task, taskState, fixerState))
482:         continue
483:       }
484: 
485:       // Phase 2: Create fix issue at threshold (same error repeated)
486:       if (taskState.retries >= FIX_ISSUE_THRESHOLD && !taskState.fixIssueNumber) {
487:         if (currentSig === taskState.errorSignature) {
488:           actions.push(createFixIssueAction(task, taskState, fixerState))
489:         } else {
490:           actions.push(createRetryAction(task, taskState, fixerState))
491:         }
492:         continue
493:       }
494: 
495:       // Phase 3: Post-fix retries
496:       if (taskState.fixIssueNumber) {
497:         actions.push(createRetryAction(task, taskState, fixerState))
498:         continue
499:       }
500: 
501:       // Fallback: retry
502:       actions.push(createRetryAction(task, taskState, fixerState))
503:     }
504: 
505:     ctx.log.info(
506:       { failedCount: failed.length, actionCount: actions.length },
507:       'Pipeline-fixer actions',
508:     )
509:     return actions
510:   },
511: }
512: 
513: // Export for testing

25: 
26: interface StageEntry {
27:   state?: string
28: }
29: 
30: interface TaskStatus {
31:   state?: string
32:   version?: number
33:   stages?: Record<string, StageEntry>

42: /**
43:  * Get the task creation date. Checks task.json first, then status.json startedAt.
44:  * Returns null if no date can be determined.
45:  */
46: function getTaskCreationDate(taskDir: string, status: TaskStatus): Date | null {
47:   // Try task.json created_at
48:   const taskJsonPath = path.join(taskDir, 'task.json')
49:   try {
50:     if (fs.existsSync(taskJsonPath)) {
51:       const data = JSON.parse(fs.readFileSync(taskJsonPath, 'utf-8')) as TaskJson
52:       if (data.created_at) {
53:         return new Date(data.created_at)
54:       }
55:     }
56:   } catch {
57:     // Ignore errors
58:   }
59: 
60:   // Fall back to status.json startedAt
61:   if (status.startedAt) {
62:     return new Date(status.startedAt)
63:   }
64: 
65:   return null
66: }

70:  *

75:  *   4. Task is not older than MAX_TASK_AGE_DAYS (staleness guard)
76:  */

78:   const stages = status.stages || {}

82:   if (!prStage || prStage.state !== 'completed') {
83:     return false

93:   const testMdPath = path.join(taskDir, 'test.md')
94:   if (fs.existsSync(testMdPath)) {
95:     return false
96:   }
97: 
98:   // Staleness guard: skip tasks older than MAX_TASK_AGE_DAYS
99:   const createdDate = getTaskCreationDate(taskDir, status)
100:   if (createdDate) {
101:     const ageMs = Date.now() - createdDate.getTime()
102:     const ageDays = ageMs / (1000 * 60 * 60 * 24)
103:     if (ageDays > MAX_TASK_AGE_DAYS) {
104:       return false
105:     }
106:   }
107: 
108:   return true
109: }
110: 
111: /**
112:  * Create an action to trigger test writing for a task via cody.yml workflow dispatch.
113:  */
114: function createDeferredTestsAction(taskId: string, _ctx: InspectorContext): ActionRequest {
115:   return {
116:     plugin: 'cody-deferred-tests',
117:     type: 'trigger-deferred-tests',
118:     target: taskId,
119:     urgency: 'info',
120:     title: `Run deferred tests for ${taskId}`,
121:     detail: `Task ${taskId} completed PR but has no test coverage. Triggering deferred test stage.`,
122:     // Dedup: don't retrigger the same task within 6 hours
123:     dedupKey: `deferred-tests:${taskId}`,
124:     dedupWindowMinutes: 360,
125:     async execute(execCtx: InspectorContext): Promise<{ success: boolean; message?: string }> {
126:       if (execCtx.dryRun) {
127:         execCtx.log.info({ taskId }, '[dry-run] Would trigger cody.yml for deferred test stage')
128:         return { success: true, message: 'dry-run: skipped' }
129:       }
130: 
131:       try {
132:         execCtx.github.triggerWorkflow('cody.yml', {
133:           task_id: taskId,
134:           mode: 'rerun',
135:           from_stage: 'test',
136:           feedback:
137:             'Deferred test run: write tests for the implemented code on dev. Create a new test branch.',
138:         })
139:         execCtx.log.info({ taskId }, 'Triggered cody.yml for deferred test stage')
140:         return { success: true, message: `Triggered deferred tests for ${taskId}` }
141:       } catch (error) {
142:         const msg = error instanceof Error ? error.message : String(error)
143:         execCtx.log.error({ taskId, error: msg }, 'Failed to trigger deferred test stage')
144:         return { success: false, message: msg }
145:       }
146:     },
147:   }
148: }
149: 
150: /**
151:  * Deferred Tests plugin — writes tests for tasks that completed the pipeline without test coverage.
152:  *
153:  * Runs every 6th cycle (~30 min) to batch up multiple completed tasks.
154:  * No complexity threshold — every task gets tests.
155:  * Staleness guard: skips tasks older than 7 days.
156:  */
157: export const deferredTestsPlugin: InspectorPlugin = {
158:   name: 'cody-deferred-tests',
159:   description: 'Trigger test writing for tasks that completed PR but have no test coverage',
160:   domain: 'cody',
161:   schedule: { every: 6 }, // Every 6th cycle = every ~30 min
162: 
163:   async run(ctx) {
164:     ctx.log.debug('Running deferred-tests plugin')
165: 
166:     const processedTasks = ctx.state.get<string[]>(STATE_KEY) || []
167:     ctx.log.debug({ processedCount: processedTasks.length }, 'Previously processed tasks')
168: 
169:     const tasksDir = path.join(process.cwd(), '.tasks')
170:     if (!fs.existsSync(tasksDir)) {
171:       return []
172:     }
173: 
174:     const entries = fs.readdirSync(tasksDir, { withFileTypes: true })
175:     const actions: ActionRequest[] = []
176: 
177:     for (const entry of entries) {
178:       if (!entry.isDirectory()) continue
179: 
180:       const taskId = entry.name
181: 
182:       // Skip already processed
183:       if (processedTasks.includes(taskId)) {
184:         ctx.log.debug({ taskId }, 'Skipping already-processed task')
185:         continue
186:       }
187: 
188:       const taskDir = path.join(tasksDir, taskId)
189:       const statusPath = path.join(taskDir, 'status.json')
190: 
191:       if (!fs.existsSync(statusPath)) continue
192: 
193:       let status: TaskStatus
194:       try {
195:         status = JSON.parse(fs.readFileSync(statusPath, 'utf-8')) as TaskStatus
196:       } catch {
197:         continue
198:       }
199: 
200:       if (!isEligibleForDeferredTests(taskDir, status)) {
201:         // If PR is completed, mark as processed so we don't keep checking
202:         const stages = status.stages || {}
203:         const prStage = stages['pr']
204:         if (prStage?.state === 'completed') {
205:           processedTasks.push(taskId)
206:           // FIX #14: Cap at 200 entries
207:           while (processedTasks.length > 200) processedTasks.shift()
208:         }
209:         continue
210:       }
211: 
212:       ctx.log.info({ taskId }, 'Task eligible for deferred tests')
213:       actions.push(createDeferredTestsAction(taskId, ctx))
214: 
215:       // Mark as processed so we only trigger once per task
216:       processedTasks.push(taskId)
217:     }
218: 
219:     ctx.state.set(STATE_KEY, processedTasks)
220: 
221:     ctx.log.debug({ actionCount: actions.length }, 'Generated deferred-tests actions')
222:     return actions
223:   },
224: }
